summary: This brief post serves as a curated index of Dr. Dotnetsky's Cool .NET Tips and Tricks series, a collection Jeff Atwood found valuable through frequent Google searches. Atwood highlights Peter Bromberg as a recurring name in .NET-related search results and recommends his alter ego's technical articles on Egghead Cafe. The post is essentially a resource compilation with light humor about missing article numbers. It was later updated when a reader found the missing issues.
key_points: Recurring expert names in search results signal reliable technical resources
Curated link collections were valuable for knowledge sharing in early blogging
Community corrections improve technical documentation over time
money_quotes: You always notice the names that appear frequently in your code related Google searches.
There are some great articles there, but the pick of the litter are the ones by Peter's alter ego, Dr. Dotnetsky.
OK, I know you're wondering: what the hell happened to issues 3, 6, 10, 13, and 15? I wish I knew. Maybe it has something to do with those drink recipes.
key_insight: Developers build trust in technical resources by noticing which expert names consistently appear in their search results.
